root_text.an4.31:1.1: “Cattārimāni, bhikkhave, cakkāni, yehi samannāgatānaṁ devamanussānaṁ catucakkaṁ vattati, yehi samannāgatā devamanussā nacirasseva mahantattaṁ vepullattaṁ pāpuṇanti bhogesu.
root_text.an4.31:1.3: Patirūpadesavāso, sappurisāvassayo, attasammāpaṇidhi, pubbe ca katapuññatā—
root_text.an4.31:1.4: Imāni kho, bhikkhave, cattāri cakkāni, yehi samannāgatānaṁ devamanussānaṁ catucakkaṁ vattati, yehi samannāgatā devamanussā nacirasseva mahantattaṁ vepullattaṁ pāpuṇanti bhogesūti.

translation_text.an4.31:1.1: “Mendicants, there are these four situations. When these situations come about, any god or human who takes advantage of them will soon acquire great and abundant wealth.
translation_text.an4.31:1.3: Living in a suitable region, relying on true persons, being rightly resolved in oneself, and past merit.
translation_text.an4.31:1.4: These are the four situations. When these situations come about, any god or human who takes advantage of them will soon acquire great and abundant wealth.
translation_text.an4.31:2.1: When a man lives in a suitable region,
translation_text.an4.31:2.4: and having merit from the past,
